jokes.text: D'oh!
jokes.setup: Opening scene with Homer
jokes.punchline: Homer's signature frustrated exclamation
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer catchphrase that perfectly encapsulates his character

jokes.text: Oh! Saturday afternoon TV is so boring.
jokes.setup: Homer complains about boring TV
jokes.punchline: While watching an actually boring sand castle preview
jokes.explanation: Relatable but not particularly clever commentary

jokes.text: Because I'm trying to reduce my boredom.
jokes.setup: Marge suggests reading to cure boredom
jokes.punchline: Homer's logical but lazy response
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer logic - circular reasoning delivered with confidence

jokes.text: Eh, I'll do it anyway.
jokes.setup: Marge explains the benefits of delivering flyers
jokes.punchline: Homer's resigned acceptance despite not caring about the benefits
jokes.explanation: Captures Homer's lazy indifference perfectly

jokes.text: The swankiest street in the classiest part of Pressboard Estates.
jokes.setup: Homer describing his neighborhood with pride
jokes.punchline: Pressboard Estates - a cheap construction material name for a supposedly classy area
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that perfectly captures suburban pretension and actual cheapness

jokes.text: It's easier. Duh!
jokes.setup: Bart questions why Homer litters if he loves the neighborhood
jokes.punchline: Homer's matter-of-fact justification for inconsistent behavior
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer - hypocritical but delivered with such confidence it's endearing

jokes.text: May I spray you with the hose in a playful fashion?
jokes.setup: Ned greeting Homer while holding a hose
jokes.punchline: Overly polite and formal way to ask about hosing someone
jokes.explanation: Peak Ned Flanders - unnecessarily formal language for a simple action

jokes.text: Eh, spray the boy.
jokes.setup: Ned's polite offer to spray Homer
jokes.punchline: Homer casually redirects to Bart instead
jokes.explanation: Efficient character moment showing Homer's casual disregard

jokes.text: I've got nothing but time until they fix that malfunctioning Squishy machine.
jokes.setup: Apu saying he has time for the rummage sale
jokes.punchline: His priorities revolve around convenience store equipment
jokes.explanation: Character-specific joke that reinforces Apu's work obsession

jokes.text: Nobody who could afford it would want to live in this neighborhood. Hey, what's wrong with this neighborhood? Big shot!
jokes.setup: Homer says nobody would want to live in his neighborhood
jokes.punchline: Immediately takes offense at his own statement
jokes.explanation: Brilliant self-contradiction - Homer arguing with himself in real time

jokes.text: Who you talking to, Homer? - The guy who doesn't live there.
jokes.setup: Marge questions who Homer is yelling at
jokes.punchline: Homer's response makes no logical sense
jokes.explanation: Absurd Homer logic - he's arguing with a hypothetical person

jokes.text: But, Marge, it works on any ayatollah- Ayatollah Nakhbadeh, Ayatollah Zahedi. As we speak, Ayatollah Razmara and his cadre of fanatics are consolidating their power.
jokes.setup: Homer defending keeping an old Ayatollah Khomeini t-shirt
jokes.punchline: Homer's unexpectedly detailed knowledge of obscure ayatollahs
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist humor - Homer randomly knowing geopolitical details

jokes.text: Oh. Well, I wanted to write 'Disco Stud,' but I ran out of space. Not that Disco Stu didn't get his share of the action.
jokes.setup: Marge questions the 'Disco Stu' name
jokes.punchline: Stu's explanation reveals both practical limitation and boastful follow-up
jokes.explanation: Great character introduction - establishes Disco Stu's vanity and practicality

jokes.text: No! Just candy, Ned. Ninety dollars!
jokes.setup: Ned suggests the candy dish could hold many nice things
jokes.punchline: Mrs. Glick's angry insistence it's only for candy at an absurd price
jokes.explanation: Perfect cranky old lady character moment with absurd specificity

jokes.text: Well, it's a moot point as I have only one tie to begin with.
jokes.setup: Discussion about motorized tie rack's practicality
jokes.punchline: Skinner's admission makes the whole debate pointless
jokes.explanation: Great Skinner moment - overwrought analysis ending in pathetic reality

jokes.text: Hey. Disco Stu doesn't advertise.
jokes.setup: Someone suggests Stu should buy the rhinestone jacket
jokes.punchline: Stu refuses to self-promote while literally wearing his name
jokes.explanation: Brilliant ironic character trait - Disco Stu IS advertising while claiming he doesn't

jokes.text: Hey, everybody! Who thinks Flanders should shut up?
jokes.setup: Ned trying to help Homer with something
jokes.punchline: Homer publicly humiliating his helpful neighbor
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer meanness toward Ned - cringe but in character

jokes.text: Say, that ayatollah thinks he's better than America. Is he right? - No! - Yes.
jokes.setup: Homer asking the crowd about the ayatollah t-shirt
jokes.punchline: Mixed response from the crowd showing divided opinion
jokes.explanation: Callback to the ayatollah t-shirt with crowd participation

jokes.text: Hey, big spender, Dig this blender, Rainbow suspenders, Hey, big spender, We surrender, Spend, Some dough at table three
jokes.setup: Musical sales pitch at the rummage sale
jokes.punchline: Increasingly ridiculous rhymes ending with surrender
jokes.explanation: Fun musical moment with decent wordplay but not exceptional

jokes.text: Disco Stu likes disco music.
jokes.setup: Bart asks what the big deal is about the new neighbor
jokes.punchline: Disco Stu's non sequitur about his musical preferences
jokes.explanation: Perfect Disco Stu - always making everything about disco

jokes.text: Hi there, neighbors. Uh, I'm George Bush.
jokes.setup: New neighbor introducing himself
jokes.punchline: Casual introduction by a former president
jokes.explanation: Good setup moment - treating celebrity like ordinary neighbor

jokes.text: Now, let's all turn around and pay attention to me again.
jokes.setup: Everyone getting excited about meeting George Bush
jokes.punchline: Homer's jealous demand for attention
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer selfishness - direct and shameless

jokes.text: Thinks just because he led the free world he can act like a big shot!
jokes.setup: Homer complaining about Bush moving in
jokes.punchline: Homer's obliviousness to why leading the free world might matter
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Homer logic - dismissing the most legitimate reason to be a big shot

jokes.text: Actually, this is one of the nine states where Mr. Bush claims residency, Dad.
jokes.setup: Homer complaining Bush should stay in his own state
jokes.punchline: Lisa's factual correction about political residency tricks
jokes.explanation: Smart political satire delivered through Lisa's knowledge

jokes.text: Wait a minute. If Lisa didn't vote for him, and I didn't vote for him- You didn't vote for anybody.
jokes.setup: Homer trying to figure out who voted for Bush
jokes.punchline: Marge's reminder that Homer doesn't vote at all
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection - Homer's logic derailed by basic fact

jokes.text: I voted for Prell to go back to the old glass bottle. After that, I became deeply cynical.
jokes.setup: Homer explaining his voting history
jokes.punchline: Homer's political cynicism stems from shampoo packaging
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist humor - Homer's political awakening tied to consumer products

jokes.text: Howdilly-doodilly there, President Bush- Or should I say President Neighbor?
jokes.setup: Ned greeting the new famous neighbor
jokes.punchline: Ned's attempt at clever wordplay with his characteristic speech pattern
jokes.explanation: Classic Ned - overly enthusiastic wordplay with signature vocabulary

jokes.text: Tiptop-notch! - Okilly-dokilly. Thankilly-dankilly. - Greatalicious. - Scrumpdiddlerific. Fine and dandy like sour candy!
jokes.setup: Bushes offering lemonade to the Flanders family
jokes.punchline: Escalating absurd Flanders-style positive responses
jokes.explanation: Great escalation of Flanders family's made-up positive words

jokes.text: So we found the town with the lowest voter turnout in America.
jokes.setup: Barbara explaining why they chose Springfield
jokes.punchline: Springfield's political apathy as a selling point
jokes.explanation: Sharp political satire about American civic engagement

jokes.text: Just happy to be here among good, average people... with no particular hopes or dreams.
jokes.setup: Barbara continuing to describe Springfield residents
jokes.punchline: Backhanded compliment about the town's lack of ambition
jokes.explanation: Brutal and accurate assessment of suburban mediocrity

jokes.text: I guess you might say he's barking up the wrong Bush.
jokes.setup: Homer watching Ned interact with Bush
jokes.punchline: Homer's proud pun on 'barking up the wrong tree' and Bush's name
jokes.explanation: Decent pun that Homer is proud of but not exceptional

jokes.text: There it is, Homer. The cleverest thing you'll ever say, and nobody heard it.
jokes.setup: Homer's pun going unnoticed
jokes.punchline: Homer's self-aware commentary on his rare moment of wit
jokes.explanation: Great meta moment - Homer recognizing his own rare cleverness

jokes.text: Oh, those cards are from Air Force One... and they only give you so many packs.
jokes.setup: Bart messes up Bush's electric card shuffler
jokes.punchline: Bush's petty concern about limited presidential playing cards
jokes.explanation: Good character detail showing Bush's petty side

jokes.text: His hands are probably covered with mud and cookies. He probably stole a napkin.
jokes.setup: Bush complaining about Bart
jokes.punchline: Escalating paranoid assumptions ending with napkin theft
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Bush's increasing paranoia about Bart

jokes.text: That's a dumb name. Who's that, George?
jokes.setup: Bush showing Bart photos
jokes.punchline: Bart's rude dismissal and relentless questioning
jokes.explanation: Typical Bart rudeness creating tension with Bush

jokes.text: Yeah? Well, welcome to the 20th century, George.
jokes.setup: Bush complaining about kids calling elders by first names
jokes.punchline: Bart's dismissive response about generational change
jokes.explanation: Good generational conflict line from Bart

jokes.text: I'll kick you right out of the 20th century, you little-
jokes.setup: Bart's sassy comeback
jokes.punchline: Bush threatening violence against a child
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Bush losing his temper

jokes.text: Oh, man. I only got one minute till they stop serving those breakfast balls! D'oh!
jokes.setup: Homer rushing somewhere
jokes.punchline: Homer's urgent concern about 'breakfast balls'
jokes.explanation: Good Homer food obsession moment

jokes.text: That's really more of a weekend thing, Ray.
jokes.setup: Burger employee suggesting Bush get a cheeseburger
jokes.punchline: Bush's inexplicable categorization of cheeseburgers
jokes.explanation: Funny arbitrary food rule that feels very Bush-like

jokes.text: That guy's louder than World War II.
jokes.setup: Homer honking his horn impatiently
jokes.punchline: Bush's comparison to a world war
jokes.explanation: Good hyperbolic comparison from someone who lived through WWII

jokes.text: Hey, my taxes paid for that horn!
jokes.setup: Secret Service telling Homer not to honk
jokes.punchline: Homer's misguided sense of ownership over government property
jokes.explanation: Homer's warped understanding of taxpayer rights and government property

jokes.text: Hey, my taxes paid for that horn!
jokes.setup: Homer being told to stop honking
jokes.punchline: Homer's misguided claim about horn ownership
jokes.explanation: Good Homer logic about public property

jokes.text: Great Scott! Don't touch that. That's the alpenhorn Helmut Kohl gave me.
jokes.setup: Bart about to touch something in Bush's house
jokes.punchline: Bush's protective concern about a gift from the German Chancellor
jokes.explanation: Good specific presidential detail

jokes.text: They're presidential pajamas. You have to be president, and you're not president. - Yes, I am. - D- No, you're not. Bar!
jokes.setup: Bush wearing special presidential pajamas
jokes.punchline: Childish argument between ex-president and actual child
jokes.explanation: Great reversal - the adult acting more childish than the child

jokes.text: Can't remember the last time she made cookies for me.
jokes.setup: Barbara offering to bake cookies for Bart
jokes.punchline: Bush's petty jealousy about his wife's attention
jokes.explanation: Good petty husband moment

jokes.text: Good, not great.
jokes.setup: Bush reading his completed memoirs
jokes.punchline: Bush's modest self-assessment of his life story
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bush understatement about his presidential legacy

jokes.text: No. Not the memoirs. Don't even think about it. Not gonna happen.
jokes.setup: Bart's destruction spreading toward Bush's memoirs
jokes.punchline: Bush's increasingly desperate protection of his book
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Bush's priorities

jokes.text: No. Not the memoirs. Don't even think about it. Not gonna happen.
jokes.setup: Motor chaos destroying things in sequence
jokes.punchline: Bush's increasing desperation to protect his written work
jokes.explanation: Building physical comedy with Bush's priorities clearly stated

jokes.text: I begged him to stop, but he said it was for the good of the nation.
jokes.setup: Bart explaining the spanking to his family
jokes.punchline: Bush's grandiose justification for spanking
jokes.explanation: Great absurd presidential justification for personal action

jokes.text: Grover Cleveland spanked me on two nonconsecutive occasions!
jokes.setup: Family discussing presidential spankings
jokes.punchline: Grandpa's historically impossible but specific memory
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Grandpa line - historically absurd but delivered with perfect specificity

jokes.text: Well, that's it!
jokes.setup: Homer listing all his grievances against Bush
jokes.punchline: Homer's dramatic decision to take action
jokes.explanation: Standard dramatic declaration

jokes.text: I'm going to punch George Bush in the face. Okay. Is he expecting you?
jokes.setup: Homer declaring violent intentions
jokes.punchline: Agent's polite bureaucratic response to threat
jokes.explanation: Great deadpan response treating assassination threat like appointment scheduling

jokes.text: I'm going to punch George Bush in the face. / Okay. Is he expecting you?
jokes.setup: Homer declaring violent intent toward former president
jokes.punchline: Secret Service treating it like a social appointment
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist response treating assassination threat as calendar scheduling

jokes.text: Whoa! Well, you are a wimp!
jokes.setup: Secret Service protecting Bush
jokes.punchline: Homer's accusation based on getting protection
jokes.explanation: Good Homer logic about why presidents get protection

jokes.text: Then you're gonna get trouble. - No, you're gonna get trouble. Oh, that's good. That's good, 'cause I want trouble.
jokes.setup: Homer and Bush agreeing to fight
jokes.punchline: Childish back-and-forth about wanting trouble
jokes.explanation: Good childish escalation between grown men

jokes.text: Two hundred bottle rockets, and George Bush doing toe touches by an open window. You get one up his butt, it's a million points.
jokes.setup: Homer planning revenge on Bush
jokes.punchline: Homer's elaborate and violent prank plan with scoring system
jokes.explanation: Dark but funny revenge fantasy with game-like scoring

jokes.text: Look, just never mind. I thought the banner was pretty straightforward, but I guess I'll just take it down.
jokes.setup: Bush trying to explain his 'Bad Neighbors' banner
jokes.punchline: Bush's frustrated resignation about miscommunication
jokes.explanation: Good frustrated Bush moment about communication failure

jokes.text: It's your sons, George Bush Jr. and Jeb Bush. Come outside, Dad.
jokes.setup: Voice at Bush's door claiming to be his sons
jokes.punchline: Obviously fake voices trying to lure Bush outside
jokes.explanation: Good obvious deception that Bush falls for

jokes.text: So I thought to myself, 'What would God do in this situation?' Locusts. They'll drive him nuts.
jokes.setup: Homer planning another prank on Bush
jokes.punchline: Homer's biblical inspiration for pest warfare
jokes.explanation: Great Homer moment connecting biblical plagues to suburban pranks

jokes.text: It's all in the Bible, son. It's the prankster's bible.
jokes.setup: Homer explaining his locust plan
jokes.punchline: Homer characterizing the Bible as a prank manual
jokes.explanation: Brilliant irreverent line reframing religious text as comedy manual

jokes.text: Oh, if he thinks George Bush'll stay out of the sewer... he doesn't know George Bush.
jokes.setup: Bush discovering Homer's sewer prank
jokes.punchline: Bush's determination to follow Homer into sewers
jokes.explanation: Good Bush escalation showing his competitive determination

jokes.text: It's just like the Noriega thing. Now he and George are the best of friends.
jokes.setup: Barbara comparing Homer-Bush fight to historical conflicts
jokes.punchline: Casual reference to Bush's invasion of Panama as friendship building
jokes.explanation: Dark political humor casually referencing military invasion as conflict resolution

jokes.text: It's just like the Noriega thing. Now he and George are the best of friends.
jokes.setup: Barbara comparing Homer and George's conflict
jokes.punchline: Casual reference to dictator Bush invaded as friendship comparison
jokes.explanation: Sharp political satire making light of military invasion through domestic analogy

jokes.text: Here's a little something we learned in C.I.A.
jokes.setup: Bush about to use fighting technique
jokes.punchline: Bush applying spy training to suburban brawl
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Bush using presidential skills for petty fight

jokes.text: Now I'll ruin you like a Japanese banquet.
jokes.setup: Homer threatening Bush during fight
jokes.punchline: Homer's confused metaphor mixing up Japanese culture
jokes.explanation: Typical Homer mangled metaphor

jokes.text: I just dropped by with present for warming of house. Instead find you grappling with local oaf.
jokes.setup: Gorbachev arriving at Bush's house
jokes.punchline: Gorbachev's matter-of-fact assessment of the situation
jokes.explanation: Good unexpected celebrity cameo with accurate assessment

jokes.text: Oh, brought some of your commie friends to help you fight dirty, eh?
jokes.setup: Gorbachev arriving to help Bush
jokes.punchline: Homer's outdated Cold War paranoia
jokes.explanation: Good Homer displaying outdated political understanding

jokes.text: But, Bar, we can't show any weakness in front of the Russians.
jokes.setup: Barbara demanding Bush apologize
jokes.punchline: Bush's Cold War mentality about suburban apology
jokes.explanation: Good Bush applying geopolitical thinking to marriage dispute

jokes.text: But, Bar, we can't show any weakness in front of the Russians. / George!
jokes.setup: Barbara demanding George apologize to Homer
jokes.punchline: Bush invoking Cold War mentality for suburban spat
jokes.explanation: Perfect satire of political paranoia applied to domestic situation

jokes.text: Whoo-hoo! In your face, Bush. Now apologize for the tax hike.
jokes.setup: Bush apologizing for spanking Bart
jokes.punchline: Homer pushing for more apologies about policy
jokes.explanation: Good Homer escalation from personal to political grievances

jokes.text: My name is Jerry Ford.
jokes.setup: New neighbor moving in after Bush leaves
jokes.punchline: Another president as replacement neighbor
jokes.explanation: Good setup for circular ending with new president

jokes.text: Say, Homer, do you like football? - Do I ever! - Do you like nachos? - Yes, Mr. Ford.
jokes.setup: Ford and Homer bonding over interests
jokes.punchline: Homer's enthusiasm building through simple pleasures
jokes.explanation: Good setup showing Homer will get along with Ford
